title: LoRa drivers accept RX calls with NULL buffers
description: Why this issue exists: lr1110_lora_recv and lora_sim_recv dereference caller-provided receive buffers without validating dev/data/size, unlike lora_loopback_recv. A bad caller or test can crash the driver instead of receiving -EINVAL. What needs to be done: add consistent NULL and zero-size validation to project LoRa recv implementations.
close_reason: Added recv argument validation to lr1110_lora_recv and lora_sim_recv so NULL dev/data and zero-size buffers return -EINVAL; verified with diff check and EC2 Zephyr gateway builds covering the project LoRa stack.
